quick_facts.label: Award and academic field
quick_facts.value: M.Sc. in Community Science is a postgraduate programme in the Community Science/Home Science domain. In ICAR’s postgraduate curriculum, it is organised around Apparel and Textile Science, Extension Education and Communication Management, Food and Nutrition, Human Development and Family Studies, and Resource Management and Consumer Science.

quick_facts.label: Usual duration
quick_facts.value: Two years is the common full-time duration reflected in university programme and admission information; applicants should confirm the structure for their target university and academic session.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry background
quick_facts.value: A four-year B.Sc. in Community Science/Home Science is a common qualifying background. Minimum marks/OGPA, accepted allied degrees, domicile rules, and specialisation prerequisites vary by university.

quick_facts.label: Admissions route
quick_facts.value: For participating agricultural universities, postgraduate admission may be routed through ICAR’s AIEEA (PG); some universities additionally use their own entrance test and merit/document-verification process.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For a learner progressing from B.Sc. (Hons.) Community Science, this degree is a direct and commonly accepted next step, but it is not a nationwide single-rule admission category. Assam Agricultural University’s published master’s eligibility includes B.Sc. (Hons.) Community Science among qualifying four-year degrees and applies stated CGPA thresholds; its prospectus also specifies that eligibility for individual Community Science specialisations can differ.

Dr. Rajendra Prasad Central Agricultural University’s published master’s admission criteria illustrate another institutional pattern: a four-year B.Sc. in Home Science or Community Science with at least 60% marks or 7.0 OGPA was required in that notice. Therefore, candidates should check the current prospectus for the exact accepted bachelor’s discipline, minimum score/OGPA, reservation and domicile provisions, and the specialisation they select.
bullets: A B.Sc. (Hons.) Community Science is an aligned feeder qualification.
Do not assume that all universities accept every allied bachelor’s degree for every specialisation.
Meet both the general PG eligibility rule and any specialisation-specific rule in the current university prospectus.

sections.heading: Duration
paragraphs: The programme is ordinarily structured as a two-year postgraduate degree. This is shown both on MGM University’s current M.Sc. Community Science programme page and in Dr. Rajendra Prasad Central Agricultural University’s published M.Sc. (Home Science) admission information. Semester pattern, dissertation/project requirements, and attendance rules remain university-specific.
bullets: Confirm the academic calendar, credit load, and dissertation/project rules with the admitting institution.

sections.heading: Curriculum and specialisations
paragraphs: There is no single identical syllabus across all Indian universities. ICAR’s revised PG Community Science curricula provide a useful sector-level reference and set out five major disciplinary streams: Apparel and Textile Science; Extension Education and Communication Management; Food and Nutrition; Human Development and Family Studies; and Resource Management and Consumer Science.

Depending on the stream and university, coursework can cover subjects such as public-health and clinical nutrition, food analysis and diet therapy; development communication and participatory programme management; textile chemistry, apparel quality and product development; human-development assessment and early-childhood interventions; or ergonomics, consumer economics, interior-space planning and environmental resource management. The ICAR material includes both theory and practical components, so candidates should compare the current course catalogue and research/dissertation requirements before choosing an institution.

sections.heading: Admission process
paragraphs: Admission routes differ by institution. ICAR has published a 2026–27 notice for PG and Ph.D. degree-programme admissions, while Dr. Rajendra Prasad Central Agricultural University states that Community Science postgraduate admissions are through ICAR-AIEEA (PG). By contrast, MGM University describes an institution-level route involving registration, its MGMU-CET, merit-list publication, document verification, and fee payment.

Applicants should treat each year’s prospectus and examination notice as controlling. Entrance-exam dates, application windows, counselling procedures, seat matrices, category rules, and document requirements can change from one admission cycle to the next.
bullets: Identify whether the target programme uses ICAR-AIEEA (PG), a university entrance test, merit selection, or a combination.
Keep qualifying-degree marksheets, identity/category/domicile documents where applicable, and specialisation eligibility evidence ready.
Use the current-year university prospectus and official examination notice rather than prior-year timelines.

sections.heading: Costs and fees
paragraphs: Fees are highly institution- and category-dependent and should be verified for the specific admission year. As an illustrative published university fee schedule, MGM University lists annual tuition of ₹30,000 for its M.Sc. Community Science programme, alongside separate annual examination and student-activity charges and one-time application, insurance/UMIS, refundable caution-deposit, eligibility, and alumni charges. This example is not a national fee benchmark and may change before admission.
bullets: Check whether quoted tuition excludes examination, hostel, mess, insurance, caution-deposit, and one-time charges.
Confirm scholarship, fee-waiver, and category-specific provisions directly with the university for the applicable session.

sections.heading: Next options
paragraphs: Graduates may continue into doctoral study in the relevant Community Science specialisation. ICAR’s accredited-programme listing includes Community Science M.Sc. specialisations and corresponding Ph.D. offerings in fields such as Apparel and Textile Science, Food and Nutrition, Extension Education and Communication Management, Human Development and Family Studies, and Resource Management and Consumer Science. ICAR also maintains a national AICE-JRF/SRF route for doctoral admissions in agriculture and allied sciences.

A suitable next step should follow the master’s specialisation and intended work: nutrition and food research, child and family studies, consumer/resource management, textiles and apparel, development communication, teaching, extension, or doctoral research. Specific job eligibility is employer- and role-dependent.
bullets: Ph.D. in a relevant Community Science/Home Science specialisation.
Research, teaching, extension, development-programme, nutrition, textile/apparel, child-development, or consumer/resource-management pathways aligned with the selected specialisation.
Track current ICAR and university doctoral admission notices for eligibility and examination requirements.
